what_it_does: Defines an image-generation system prompt that maps a numeric {warmth} value to warm, cool, or balanced visual direction, with material, lighting, palette, composition, and negative-prompt constraints.
observations: States that warmth should be carried by the prompt rather than by layout or a flat color gradient.
Defines WARM for warmth >= 60 as a golden-hour architectural interior register.
Defines COOL for warmth <= 40 as a flat, near-white mineral composition register.
Defines BALANCED for 40 < warmth < 60 as interpolation between warm and cool guidance.
Uses repeated constraints around matte mineral or organic materials, sparse gold, calm spacing, and one focal point.
Includes negative prompts against gradients, digital lighting, gloss, neon, saturation, clutter, cartoon style, HDR, stock-photo look, watermark, and text.
standouts: Warmth thresholds drive distinct visual registers.
The prompt explicitly rejects flat gradient warmth and asks the image prompt to carry the warmth instead.
Material and lighting language is tightly constrained around matte, mineral, near-white, and sparse metallic accents.
Global ALWAYS and NEVER constraints summarize the shared style envelope.
conventions: Uses all-caps section labels and separator rules for prompt structure.
Pairs positive visual direction with NEGATIVE constraints.
Uses threshold-based conditional prose rather than executable configuration.
Uses typographic symbols and punctuation for visual organization.
concerns: The COOL negative prompt references "same as above", which depends on nearby context if the section is reused alone.
The BALANCED section describes interpolation qualitatively without specifying exact weighting or phrasing rules.
notes: Brief this as a warmth-controlled image prompt for restrained architectural and mineral visuals.
questions: How should interpolation be calibrated within the balanced 40-60 range?
Is {warmth} always supplied as a numeric value before this prompt is used?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches what it does: it is an image-generation system prompt organized around warmth-controlled visual registers.
apparent_themes: negative constraints against synthetic or busy imagery
intention_signals: It seems intended to make generated imagery feel warm or restrained through lighting, material, and composition language while avoiding obvious digital gradients or saturated visual treatments.
novelty: The file makes warmth a semantic image-prompt responsibility rather than a layout or color-gradient responsibility.
contribution: Contributes a prompt-level visual language for warmth-controlled architectural imagery, including thresholds, register-specific direction, shared material constraints, and reusable negative constraints.
summary_for_embedding: An image-generation system prompt that controls architectural/mineral visual style through a 0-100 warmth parameter with warm, cool, and balanced registers plus positive and negative constraints.
intention_for_embedding: This was for translating a warmth setting into restrained image-generation guidance through lighting, materials, palette, spacing, and anti-pattern constraints.
